title: 16S rRNA gene Library Preparation Protocol
author: dinesh.aggarwal
materials: Set Up the MPure 12 Reagents and plasticware: Place the reagent cartridge in the large flat block at the back, it should click into place, and the writing and release flap should be facing toward you. The clear plastic mixing wells are clipped in front of these. Then place the black plastic container set into the next slots, into the first of these place a plastic pin, in the third place a tip. Put an open labelled elution tube in the closest holes in the metal rack (labelled E).
UV the MPure machine before use.

steps.title: Thaw and vortex samples
steps.instruction: Remove samples to be extracted from the freezer, to thaw in the fridge ( 4 °C ). Once thawed vortex for 00:00:10 . Proceed in MSC.

steps.title: Transfer sample to Eppendorf tube
steps.instruction: Pipette mix and remove up to 800 µL from the storage tube and transfer to a labelled 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend pellet
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all bar 100 µL of the supernatant. Resuspend the bacterial pellet in the remaining liquid.

steps.title: Transfer to lysis tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the volume to a labelled lysis tube ( Lysing Matrix E, MP Biomedicals). Label the side of the tubes.

steps.title: Lyse samples in FastPrep
steps.instruction: Place lysis tubes in the ‘FastPrep Instrument’ and run for 00:00:40 at speed 6000.

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to MPure sample tubes
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant ( 450 µL, or as close as possible ) from each lysis tube to a labelled blue sample tube from MPure bacterial DNA extraction kit. Place these in the metal rack, in the slots labelled “S” on the machine.

steps.title: Run MPure DNA extraction
steps.instruction: Machine instructions: Select “START”, it will scan the setup from the sheet in the kit. Select the protocol : Mpure Bacterial DNA extraction kit with barcode: “OP02006”· Select sample volume 400ul with barcode ”SV0400”· Select elution volume 50ul with barcode “EV0050”· Press Enter to start the extraction The run time is around 1 hour. The machine will stall if an error develops, but will not stall if a tip is lost, or plasticware comes unclipped.

steps.title: Store eluted DNA
steps.instruction: Remove elution tubes, checking a reasonable volume is present, and store these at -80 °C in a labelled box.

steps.title: Clean work area with DNAZap
steps.instruction: Clean MSC or PCR hood with DNAZap.

steps.title: Dilute primers
steps.instruction: Dilute primers to working concentration ( 10 millimolar (mM), the stock is kept at 100mM ,) in NFW, if there are no pre-existing dilutions < 2 weeks old.

steps.title: Aliquot and store primers
steps.instruction: Aliquot diluted primers into labelled, linked PCR tubes. Freeze upright overnight if necessary.

steps.title: Prepare and store library samples and controls
steps.instruction: Rack out the samples for the library into 12 x 8 rack according to use, complete plate plan, put on lid, label and store in -80°C. Remember to include: - PCR negative control(s) from the relevant DNA extractions - positive control (mock community or bacterrial DNA) - negative PCR control (nuclease free water). - any diluted, or pre-spiked samples.

steps.title: Final cleaning and UV
steps.instruction: Wipe down MSC with Distel/DNAZap, and then 80% ethanol. Wipe down all equipment with distel and put in the MSC. Close MSC, and UV.

steps.title: Add nuclease free water to master mix
steps.instruction: Add 14.25 µL, per well nuclease free water to labelled master mix tubes.

steps.title: Add Q5 buffer to master mix
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, per well Q5 buffer (Q5 High-Fidelity Polymerase Kit from New England Biolabs) to the master mix tubes. Mix before use, vortexing if salts have fallen from solution.
